claim: AutoGen's default GroupChatManager cannot consistently enforce function calls when agents have functions; a custom orchestrator class is necessary for reliable function invocation
actionable: When using AutoGen for multi-agent systems with function calling, build a custom orchestrator that explicitly checks for function availability and enforces execution rather than relying on GroupChatManager
source_title: Make AutoGen Consistent: CONTROL your LLM agents for ACCURATE Postgres AI Data Analytics

claim: Sequential conversation flow (A→B→C) and broadcast conversation flow (A broadcasts to B,C,D in parallel) are distinct architectural patterns that should be implemented explicitly rather than relying on framework defaults
actionable: Design multi-agent systems by specifying conversation topology explicitly (sequential vs broadcast) and implement custom logic for each; don't assume frameworks provide optimal routing
source_title: Make AutoGen Consistent: CONTROL your LLM agents for ACCURATE Postgres AI Data Analytics

claim: Keeping agent conversation history in memory-augmented structures enables debugging and analysis; separating message-passing logic from function-call logic improves orchestration reliability
actionable: Maintain explicit message logs separate from agent memory; differentiate between 'basic chat' (message passing), 'memory chat' (agent stores its own reply), and 'function chat' (handles function invocation)
source_title: Make AutoGen Consistent: CONTROL your LLM agents for ACCURATE Postgres AI Data Analytics

claim: With OpenAI function calls, the LLM must first suggest the function call, then execute it, then pass results downstream; ignoring this two-step pattern breaks function invocation in agent conversations
actionable: When orchestrating function calls in multi-agent systems, implement a two-phase handler: first trigger the suggestion, then execute, then forward the result
source_title: Make AutoGen Consistent: CONTROL your LLM agents for ACCURATE Postgres AI Data Analytics

claim: Agent-specific function maps and LLM configurations can be composed from a base configuration to reduce duplication and enable per-agent capability control
actionable: Build a hierarchical configuration structure where each agent inherits from a base config and overlays its own specific function map; avoid duplicating tool definitions
source_title: Make AutoGen Consistent: CONTROL your LLM agents for ACCURATE Postgres AI Data Analytics

claim: Not controlling agent conversation flow leaves you 'up to the mercy' of framework constraints; building custom orchestrators is necessary for production multi-agent systems
actionable: Always build custom orchestration logic for multi-agent systems rather than accepting framework defaults; treat conversation topology as a first-class design decision
source_title: Make AutoGen Consistent: CONTROL your LLM agents for ACCURATE Postgres AI Data Analytics
